name: Old English Sheepdog
description: The Old English Sheepdog, with its profuse shaggy coat and distinctive shuffling gait, is an iconic British breed originally developed in the 19th century to drive cattle and sheep to market. These amiable, adaptable dogs are devoted to their families and show a playful, gentle side with children, making them excellent household companions despite their large size. Their magnificent double coat requires extensive daily brushing to prevent matting, making grooming a serious commitment for prospective owners.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4
